zh.Arkel, Giessenlanden, South Holland, Net: 荷蘭-拉藍德希森蘭登阿可爾

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan I van Arkel於1233年1月7日出生在荷蘭-拉藍德阿可爾時，他的父親Herbaren II van Arkel29歲，母親Aleidis van Heusden42歲。他於1258年與Bertha van Ochten在荷蘭蓋爾德藍德奧特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1253年被列為heer van arkel。他去世於1272年5月15日在荷蘭-拉藍德阿可爾享年39歲，埋葬在荷蘭霍蘭格利姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan I van Arkel于1233年1月7日出生在荷兰-拉藍德阿可爾时，他的父亲Herbaren II van Arkel29岁，母亲Aleidis van Heusden42岁。他于1258年与Bertha van Ochten在荷兰蓋爾德藍德奧特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1253年被列为heer van arkel。他去世于1272年5月15日在荷兰-拉藍德阿可爾享年39岁，埋葬在荷兰霍蘭格利姆。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Arkel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Breton: from the Old Breton personal name Arthmael, composed of the elements arth ‘bear’ (in a transferred sense ‘soldier’) + mael ‘prince’, Latinized as Armagilus. This was the name of a Christian saint (5th century) who was the subject of a local cult in Brittany.
Jewish (from Ukraine): artificial name from Yiddish dialect (h)arml ‘hermine’.

variants.name: Ermel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Jurgen, Markus, Matthias.

German: from Middle High German ermel ‘sleeve’, a metonymic occupational name for a sleeve maker. Alternatively, it may have been a nickname for someone who wore clothing with conspicuous sleeves; in medieval times the size of sleeves was regulated by luxury laws.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Erm, from erm, irm ‘all-encompassing, great’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace). Compare Armel 1.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Jan I van Arkel was born on 7 January 1233, in Arkel, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Herbaren II van Arkel, was 29 and his mother, Aleidis van Heusden, was 42. He married Bertha van Ochten in 1258, in Ochten, Gelderland, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. In 1253, his occupation is listed as heer van arkel. He died on 15 May 1272, in Arkel, South Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 39, and was buried in Gorinchem, Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan I van Arkel est né le 7 janvier 1233, à Arkel, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Herbaren II van Arkel, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Aleidis van Heusden, avait 42 ans. Il a épousé Bertha van Ochten en 1258, à Ochten, Gueldre, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. En 1253, sa profession de son indiquée est heer van arkel. Il est décédé le 15 mai 1272, à Arkel, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Gorinchem, Holland, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jan I van Arkel am 7. Januar 1233 in Arkel, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herbaren II van Arkel 29 und seine Mutter Aleidis van Heusden 42. Er hat 1258 in Ochten, Geldern, Niederlande Bertha van Ochten geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1253 ist sein Beruf als heer van arkel aufgeführt. Er starb am 15. Mai 1272 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Arkel, Südholland, Niederlande und wurde in Gorinchem, Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Arkel è nato il il 7 gennaio 1233 nel Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Herbaren II van Arkel, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Aleidis van Heusden, aveva 42 anni. Lui sposato Bertha van Ochten nel 1258, nel Ochten, Gelderland, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1253, la sua occupazione è indicata come heer van arkel. Morì il 15 maggio 1272, nel Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Gorinchem, Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan I van Arkelが1233年1月7日にオランダズイド-hオランドアルケルで生まれた時，父Herbaren II van Arkelは29歳，母Aleidis van Heusdenは42歳であった。1258年にオランダジェルダーランドオチテンでBertha van Ochtenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1253年，職業はheer van arkelと記録されている。彼は1272年5月15日にオランダズイド-hオランドアルケルで39歳で死去し，オランダホーランドゴリンケムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan I van Arkel(이)가 1233년 1월 7일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 아클에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herbaren II van Arkel(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Aleidis van Heusden(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1258년 네덜란드 글델랜드 오트턴에서 Bertha van Ochten(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1253년, 그의 직업은 heer van arkel이었다. 그는 1272년 5월 15일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 아클에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 홀랜드 고린체에 묻혔다.

pt.7. Januar 1233–15 May 1272: 7 de janeiro de 1233–15 de maio de 1272
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van Arkel nasceu em 7 de janeiro de 1233, em Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Herbaren II van Arkel, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Aleidis van Heusden, tinha 42 anos. Ele casou-se com Bertha van Ochten em 1258, em Ochten, Gheldria, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1253, sua profissão aparece como heer van arkel. Ele faleceu em 15 de maio de 1272, em Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Gorinchem, Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan I van Arkel родился 7 января 1233 г. в местности аркел, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Herbaren II van Arkel было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Aleidis van Heusden, 42. Он женился на женщине по имени Bertha van Ochten в 1258 г. в местности очтен, джелдирланд, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1253 году он работал по профессии heer van arkel. Он умер 15 мая 1272 г. в местности аркел, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности горинчем, холланд, Нидерланды.

sm.from 1253 to 1272: mai le 1253 e o'o I le 1272

es.7. Januar 1233–15 May 1272: 7 de enero de 1233–15 de mayo de 1272
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jan I van Arkel nació el 7 de enero de 1233, en Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Herbaren II van Arkel, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Aleidis van Heusden, tenía 42 años. Se casó con Bertha van Ochten en 1258, en Ochten, Gelderland, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. En 1253, su ocupación registrada era heer van arkel. Murió el 15 de mayo de 1272, en Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Gorinchem, Holland, Países Bajos.
